mission.title: opentelemetry-collector: otlphttp exporter drops data because memory limiter (of another instance) is considered as non recoverable error
mission.description: otlphttp exporter drops data because memory limiter (of another instance) is considered as non recoverable error. This issue affects 8+ users.

steps.title: Identify opentelemetry-collector troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your opentelemetry-collector deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n opentelemetry-collector -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opentelemetry-collector
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opentelemetry-collector -n opentelemetry-collector --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review opentelemetry-collector configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant opentelemetry-collector configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n opentelemetry-collector -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opentelemetry-collector
kubectl get configmap -n opentelemetry-collector -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=opentelemetry-collector
```
Data are being dropped by exporter, because receiver is throttled by memory limiter. This is incorrect in my opinion as receiver may be behind load balancer, so after retry packet may be forwarded to healthy instance.

steps.title: Apply the fix for otlphttp exporter drops data because memory limiter (of…
steps.description:
Updates the receiver's http response to return a proper http status based on whether or not the pipeline returned a retryable error.  Builds upon the work done in https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-collector/pull/8080 and
```yaml
exporters:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            
  otlphttp:                                                                                                                                                                      
```

steps.title: Confirm otlphttp exporter drops data because memory… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opentelemetry-collector -n opentelemetry-collector --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n opentelemetry-collector --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
Updates the receiver's http response to return a proper http status based on whether or not the pipeline returned a retryable error.  Builds upon the work done in https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-collector/pull/8080 and https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-collector/pull/9307


Updated lots of unit tests
codeSnippets:
otelCollector:
  name: "otel-collector"
  image:
    registry: docker.io
    repository: signoz/signoz-otel-collector
    tag: 0.79.4

error	exporterhelper/queued_retry.go:391	Exporting failed. The error is not retryable. Dropping data.	{"kind": "exporter", "data_type": "traces", "name": "otlp", "error": "Permanent error: rpc error: code = Unknown desc = data refused due to high memory usage", "dropped_items": n}

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with opentelemetry-collector installed or the issue environment reproducible.
